evidence: Task fully specified by DEFERRED.md #003: save_state non-atomic write → crash mid-write corrupts .workflow-state.json. Fix scope: workflow-gate.py save_state() only. No open questions.

Atomic-write idiom: serialize to STATE_FILE.with_name(name+'.tmp') in same directory, then tmp.replace(STATE_FILE). Path.replace() = atomic rename on same filesystem (os.replace/MoveFileEx); handles dest-exists on Windows. Crash before replace → STATE_FILE untouched (old state preserved); leftover .tmp harmless (next write overwrites).

V1 5 repeated writes → valid JSON + dest-exists replace OK; V2 no .tmp leftover; V3 tmp same-dir as STATE_FILE (atomic-rename precondition); V4 non-serializable state → json.dumps raises → STATE_FILE preserved unchanged (crash-safety property confirmed)

note: Fixed .tmp name races concurrent invocations; cwd-relative tmp breaks same-dir guarantee if cwd changes; Windows PermissionError on locked dest + leftover .tmp never cleaned.

DEFERRED #002: cmd_complete bridge uses substring 'REJECTED' in evidence.upper() — false-positives on 'NOT REJECTED' etc. DEFERRED suggested word-boundary regex, but \bREJECTED\b still matches 'NOT REJECTED'. Correct fix: read the structured status field from AUDIT_LOG.jsonl adversary review events, not free-text evidence.

Add _had_rejected_review(task_slug) helper: scan AUDIT_LOG.jsonl, return True if any event with action=review AND status=REJECTED for this task. cmd_complete bridge condition for review-design/review-code becomes _had_rejected_review(task_slug) instead of substring match. Structured signal — authoritative, immune to evidence free-text wording.

_had_rejected_review verified 5 cases: A no-log→False; B review-REJECTED→True; C approved-only→False; D phase_complete event with 'REJECTED' in free-text evidence→NOT matched (action!=review, the exact #002 false-positive); E r1-REJECTED+r2-APPROVED→True (rejection captured). Mirror byte-identical, syntax OK.

DEFERRED #004: cmd_amaw_pre_commit calls load_state() which auto-creates .workflow-state.json from INITIAL_STATE if absent. When an agent commits outside any tracked task, the &&-chained amaw-pre-commit hook creates a stale state file → confusing [ ] markers on next status. Fix: early-exit cmd_amaw_pre_commit if STATE_FILE absent, before load_state().

Add STATE_FILE.exists() guard at top of cmd_amaw_pre_commit: if absent → sys.exit(0) silent (no task in flight = nothing to gate). Mirrors cmd_pre_commit's own no-state early-exit. load_state() auto-create behaviour left unchanged (other commands rely on it).

No state file → amaw-pre-commit exits 0 + creates NO state file (#004 bug fixed — verified by ls check). With state file + amaw_enabled → still runs check_guardrails (CLEAR, 3 rules). Mirror byte-identical, syntax OK.
